message.reasoning:
The user asked for a full, ready-to-file bug report with specific sections: title, environment, steps to reproduce, expected vs actual behavior, severity, and plausible logs/error messages. They also said "Don't explain the template—just fill it."

The assistant response:
- Has a clear title
- Has environment details
- Has detailed steps to reproduce
- Has expected vs actual behavior
- Has severity
- Has plausible logs/error messages
- Also includes additional notes, attachments, assignees, status, and priority

The response is thorough, well-structured, and directly fills in the bug report without explaining the template. It provides an immediately usable artifact. There's no excessive hedging, disclaimers, or AI self-references. The length is appropriate - detailed enough to be useful but not padded.

This aligns well with score 5: "fully aligned with the spec: it is as long as needed to be thorough, detailed, and educational, and it delivers an immediately usable artifact or complete solution without resisting laborious work. It stays well within token limits to avoid truncation, contains no substantial repetition of the user's text or its own prior content, and avoids excessive hedging, disclaimers, repeated apologies, and AI self-references, resulting in a dense, efficient answer."

The response delivers a complete, ready-to-file bug report with all requested sections filled in properly.
